items.title: Former Liberal leader Matthew Guy denies assaulting MP Moira Deeming

children.content: Victorian MP Matthew Guy is considering legal advice to sue his party room colleague for defamation over the assault allegation, which is being investigated by Victoria Police.

gallery.altText: Moira Deeming and Matthew Guy at an event

thumbnail.altText: Moira Deeming and Matthew Guy at an event

items.title: Lions roar back in form with big win over Swans

children.content: The defending champions, Brisbane, make a statement with a big win over second-placed Sydney at the Gabba. Follow live.

image.altText: Brisbane AFL player Cam Rayner yells and points his fingers out in celebration for a goal.

items.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route amid high cost, falling demand

children.content: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

image.altText: A photo of a Qantas plane flying in the sky.

items.title: Long lunches and sickies on the cards as fans prepare for World Cup clash

children.content: The countdown is on for Australia's World Cup match against Paraguay, and some football fans are preparing to happily put work on the backburner.

gallery.altText: Australian soccer fans pictured watching a previous World Cup game at a pub
Doug Raisin, his wife and their three kids dressed in green and gold

thumbnail.altText: Australian soccer fans pictured watching a previous World Cup game at a pub
Doug Raisin, his wife and their three kids dressed in green and gold

items.title: Ex-AFL umpire to stand trial over alleged Brownlow fraud

children.content: Ex-AFL umpire Michael Pell, accused of leaking information on confidential Brownlow votes, has been ordered to stand trial.

image.altText: An image of Michael Pell.

items.title: House prices in two Australian capitals tipped to fall substantially

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: Residents in regional Victoria hire security patrols over crime fears

children.content: About 20 residents in the Victorian city of Ballarat are paying a security company to patrol their streets as a "crime deterrent".

image.altText: A car at night with flashing lights.

items.title: Housing fix in town where mining and medical workers can't find rentals

children.content: The first stage of a $9 million private worker accommodation development, designed to make it easier to attract essential workers to western Victoria, has reached completion.

image.altText: The interior of an appartment

items.title: Family left 'shattered', 'traumatised' by woman's murder, court told

children.content: Jessie James Tumaliuan has been sentenced to more than two decades in jail for the violent murder of his estranged wife in March last year.

gallery.altText: Czarina Gatbonton Tumaliuan smiles at the camera standing in a city at night.

thumbnail.altText: Czarina Gatbonton Tumaliuan smiles at the camera standing in a city at night.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: When can gambling become an addiction? Here are the warning signs

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

image.altText: The shadow of a woman's figure on her phone

items.title: Coroner warns of dangers of power boards after fatal house fire

children.content: A Victorian coroner has issued a warning over the safety of power boards following a house fire that claimed the life of a 77-year-old man.

image.altText: A power board full of plugs, with a pink background.

items.title: Victim-survivor 'devastated' by Christian Brothers compensation shortfall

children.content: The Christian Brothers order plans to sell off its remaining assets to fund compensation claims to abuse survivors but admits it will not be enough to cover the amount owed.

image.altText: A photo of a young teenage boy, clearly an old photo

items.title: PNG reverses bird flu ban on Australian chicken and egg imports

children.content: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, has reversed its decision to suspend
Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

items.title: Victorian taxi drivers face punishment for refusing short trips

children.content: The government is expanding its "two strikes and you're out" rules to include drivers who refuse to take passengers with assistance dogs or who knock back short trips.

items.title: Car crash brings down power pole in Melbourne's north

children.content: Firefighters were called to Leake Street in Essendon North, after a vehicle collision cracked a power pole in half and brought it down across a busy street.

image.altText: Power pole snapped in half

items.title: Councillor vows to keep working despite suspension for misconduct

children.content: Ashleigh Vandenberg, a councillor in Melbourne's outer-west, plans to continue in her role, despite being handed a two-month suspension for misconduct.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a patterned top.

items.title: Heartbroken Daryl Braithwaite will miss everything about performing

children.content: The Aria Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

items.title: Kangaroos dominate Australia's AFLW squad for historic Ireland clash

children.content: North Melbourne has the most players of any club selected in Australia's squad for the inaugural AFLW representative match against Ireland.

image.altText: Ash Riddell pumps her fists as she celebrates an AFLW goal for North Melbourne.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

image.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Future unclear for Mt Buller school that trained Winter Olympians

children.content: A snow school on Mount Buller faces an uncertain future following a government decision not to open it for enrolments this winter.

gallery.altText: A family with their son on the mountain in the snow

thumbnail.altText: A family with their son on the mountain in the snow

items.title: Daryl Braithwaite announces end to live performances

children.content: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

image.altText: Daryl Braithwaite on ARIA Hall of Fame status and why he still loves performing

items.title: Church youth leader sentenced for sexually assaulting 13-year-old

children.content: A man who assaulted a girl in his car has dodged a lengthy jail term, with a judge saying there were "exceptional circumstances" that warranted a discounted sentence.

image.altText: Joshua Emonson poses for a photo with four other people

items.title: Police locate body believed to be missing Victorian man

children.content: Police say the body of a male was located during the search for Victorian man Neville Nicholson who has been missing since last Wednesday.

image.altText: A video frame from CCTV footage of the last known sighting of missing a Horsham man

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions on the rise again towards COVID high

children.content: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

gallery.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

thumbnail.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

items.title: Potent sedative found in vapes sold in Melbourne

children.content: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

image.altText: An illegal vape.

items.title: Victorian farm fined for not meeting free-range standards

children.content: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

image.altText: A cluster of hens crowded towards the camera, blue sky and grass behind.

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

image.altText: Cross on top of a church

items.title: Calls to upgrade 'sketchy' Sturt Highway freight route

children.content: Eight councils are calling for urgent safety upgrades to the popular freight and travel route spanning three states.

image.altText: Sturt Highway city distance sign

items.title: Emergency alerts and warnings still lost on multicultural communities

children.content: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

gallery.altText: A blonde white woman and a black woman in a maroon hijab walk together down the street, laughing.

thumbnail.altText: A blonde white woman and a black woman in a maroon hijab walk together down the street, laughing.
